title: Walsh credits recent initiatives for a drop in crime in Syracuse
summary: Mayor Walsh reports a 23.7% overall crime reduction in Syracuse year-over-year, with violent crime down 19% and property crime down 24.6%. He attributes success to the Syracuse Police Department and eight years of city initiatives including the Mayor's Office to Reduce Gun Violence, Safer Streets program, and Police/Athletic Activities League. The announcement comes as Walsh prepares to leave office, with Mayor-Elect Owens expected to continue these efforts.

entity_refs.name: Mayor's Office to Reduce Gun Violence

political_signal.description: Outgoing Mayor Walsh champions crime reduction as policy success, positioning administration's public safety initiatives as model for other communities and establishing positive baseline for successor administration.
